repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem:
Terminal buffer content and metadata are lost once the terminal
process exits.

Solution:
- Saving as plain text uses `:w name_without_mpack_extention.txt`:
  Keep plain `:write` as a normal buffer text write, so no existing
  behavior is lost.
- Saving as rendered mpack uses `:w name_with_mapck.mapck`:
  Use the `.mpack` extension to opt into exporting rendered terminal
  state as msgpack with metadata.
  - User passes only filename:
    save it under `stdpath('state')/term/<name>.mpack`.
  - User passes filename containing path separators:
    directly save it to that specific path.


ref https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/40017

comment_data.body: this is fine as a temporary approach but let's plan to revisit later, see https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/40017#issuecomment-4623175666

saving as a string isn't great, it loses information (quoting rules are nasty, and `shell_argv_to_str` doesn't really even attempt to handle complex quoting...)

this makes me think it would be worthwhile to move `terminal_pack_content` into Lua. from `src/nvim/terminal.c`, you can call `nlua_call_vimfn` with a manually-constructed `typval_T`. [example](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/03f746e913904ea8291d13ac7236f65a8c1eec26/src/nvim/help.c#L337-L342)
moving this to Lua also is worthwhile

Could we augment `:write` so that it handles terminal buffers? I.e. `:write` does what `:termsave` does, then we don't need `:termsave`.

And as a bonus, user can do `:write foo.mpack` if they want to save it somewhere else.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -265,6 +267,15 @@ Example: >vim
 Use |jobwait()| to check if the terminal job has finished: >vim
     let running = jobwait([&channel], 0)[0] == -1
 <
+
+==============================================================================
+Terminal persistence                                    *terminal-persistence*
+
+                                                                   *:termsave*
+:termsave       Save the current terminal buffer state to a file in

comment_data.body:
need docstring.

let's use a very obvious, common prefix for all functions related to "serializing the terminal state". maybe `te_encode`.

or maybe these should all live in a separate file, `terminal_encode.c`. that will reduce merge-conflicts later, if/when the libghostty refactor lands.


```suggestion
static void te_serialize_sgr_color(StringBuilder *out, const VTermColor *color, bool is_fg,
```

It's not completely correct here, as terminal buffers can be opened in 2 ways and they are different in `streamtype` (`kChannelStreamProc` and `kChannelStreamInternal`).
`.cwd` is not completely correct either. It doesn't reflect `cd` inside the terminal. I believe the "OSC 7" sequence will be helpful here. THe implementation should be like `b:term_title`. However, I'm curious why this haven't been implemented yet, the current `cwd` is not reliable. Is there any concern?
This is a start. We need more metadata than this in the future, for example, cursor position (cursor row and col).
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,82 @@
+local M = {}
+
+--- Saves a terminal buffer's rendered state and metadata as a msgpack file.
+---
+--- The destination is based on `fname`:
+---   - a bare name is stored under stdpath('state')/term/<name>.mpack;
+---   - a name containing a path separator is written to that path.
+---
+---@param buf_handle integer terminal buffer handle (source of argv).
+---@param ansi       string ANSI escape sequences of the terminal state/scrollback content.
+---@param cwd        string current working directory of the shell.
+---@param fname      string user-specified name.
+---@param force      boolean overwrite an existing destination (`:write!`).
+---@param mkdir_p    boolean create missing parent directories for explicit paths (`++p`).
+---@return table     result { true, msg } / { false, err, path }
+function M.save(buf_handle, ansi, cwd, fname, force, mkdir_p)
+  if fname:sub(-6) ~= '.mpack' then
+    fname = fname .. '.mpack' --[[@as string]]
+  end
+
+  -- Classify the user-specified name:
+  --   - contains a path separator:
+  --     treat as a real path and write there directly.
+  --     (e.g. `:write ./foo.mpack`, `:write /tmp/foo.mpack`).
+  --   - is a bare name:
+  --     store under stdpath('state')/term/<name>.mpack.
+  local des ---@type string
+  local explicit_path = fname:find('[/\\]') ~= nil
+  if explicit_path then
+    des = vim.fn.fnamemodify(vim.fs.normalize(fname), ':p')
+  else
+    des = vim.fs.joinpath(vim.fn.stdpath('state'), 'term', fname)
+  end
+
+  local stat = vim.uv.fs_stat(des)
+  if stat and stat.type == 'directory' then
+    return { ok = false, err = 17, path = des }
+  end
+  -- Overwrite policy.
+  local existed = stat ~= nil
+  if existed and not force then
+    return { ok = false, err = 13, path = des }
+  end
+
+  local chan = vim.bo[buf_handle].channel
+  local info = vim.api.nvim_get_chan_info(chan)
+
+  local packed = vim.mpack.encode({
+    cwd = cwd,
+    argv = info.argv,
+    timestamp = vim.fn.localtime(),
+    content = ansi,
+  })

comment_data.body: I'm thinking if it's a good design. All other `:w foo` writes `foo` file to the cwd. This unexpected behavior may surprise users I think? Maybe all `:w` mpack files should NOT be saved to `stdpath('state')/term/`. Maybe when we add `autosave` for terminal mpack files, should this path take into consideration.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -265,6 +265,30 @@ Example: >vim
 Use |jobwait()| to check if the terminal job has finished: >vim
     let running = jobwait([&channel], 0)[0] == -1
 <
+==============================================================================
+Terminal persistence                                    *terminal-persistence*
+
+In a terminal buffer, `:write` with a ".mpack" extension exports the rendered
+terminal state (scrollback + visible screen) as a msgpack file, including
+metadata such as cwd, argv, and timestamp.
+
+`:w {name}.mpack`           Save rendered terminal state as a msgpack file to
+                          `stdpath('state')/term/`.

comment_regular_data.body:
~I'll investigate if the flaky CI error is relevant to the code implementation.~
And I plan to refactor the exported function a little bit -- I am not quite satisfied with the current implementation, as it outputs some redundant ansi escape codes, making the content in the export file bigger.
